description: Integrates nursing science with knowledge from ethics, the biophysical, psychosocial, analytical, and organizational sciences as the basis for practice.

competencies.text: Demonstrate an understanding of the discipline of nursing and its meta-paradigm.
Apply theory and research-based knowledge from nursing, the arts, humanities, and other sciences.
Demonstrate clinical reasoning connecting the art and science of nursing.
Translate evidence from nursing science and other disciplines to inform practice and make practice decisions.
Integrate an understanding of nursing history and the foundational role of nursing in influencing professional development, societal advancement, and healthcare change.

description: Integrates care, knowledge, and skills to work with persons, families, populations, and communities across the lifespan.

competencies.text: Engage with the individual in establishing a caring relationship.
Communicate effectively with individuals.
Integrate assessment skills to inform clinical decision-making.
Diagnose actual or potential health problems and needs.
Develop a plan of care.
Demonstrate accountability for care delivery.
Evaluate and revise plan based on individual's response.
Promote self-care management.
Provide end-of-life care.

description: Applies population health concepts to analyze and address health disparities, determinants of health, and advocate for health equity.

competencies.text: Manage care of populations.
Engage in ethical decision-making regarding population health.
Participate in development and implementation of advocacy strategies to address health disparities.
Apply epidemiological principles to inform population health.
Advance equitable and ethical population-based policies, interventions, and services.
Communicate population health data with team members and communities.

description: Applies principles of evidence-based practice and nursing research to advance the discipline and improve care.

competencies.text: Advance the scholarship of nursing.
Integrate best evidence into nursing practice.
Promote the ethical conduct of research and scholarship.
Apply research findings to practice.

description: Applies quality improvement and safety science to provide safe, effective, efficient, equitable, timely, and patient-centered care.

competencies.text: Apply quality improvement methods.
Contribute to a culture of patient safety.
Participate in activities that improve patient outcomes.
Use quality and safety concepts to guide clinical practice.

description: Collaborates with interprofessional teams to optimize care delivery and healthcare outcomes through teamwork.

competencies.text: Communicate effectively within interprofessional teams.
Perform effectively in different team roles.
Apply teamwork principles to interprofessional collaboration.
Demonstrate mutual respect among all members of the healthcare team.

description: Analyzes complex organizational systems to improve care delivery, reduce errors, and advance health outcomes.

competencies.text: Apply knowledge of systems to work effectively across the continuum of care.
Incorporate consideration of cost and economic factors in care delivery.
Optimize system effectiveness through application of innovation and evidence-based practice.
Apply knowledge of regulatory requirements and standards.

description: Uses clinical informatics, data management, and healthcare technologies to coordinate care and improve outcomes.

competencies.text: Describe the rationale for the use of technology in providing care.
Use technology to document care, communicate, and coordinate care.
Use informatics and evidence-based information to support practice decisions.
Support consumers in accessing information.

description: Demonstrates professional identity, values, comportment, and accountability consistent with nursing practice.

competencies.text: Demonstrate an ethical comportment in one's practice reflective of nursing's mission to society.
Employ a participatory approach to nursing care.
Demonstrate accountability to the individual, society, and the profession.
Comply with relevant laws, regulations, and policies.
Demonstrate the professional identity of nursing.
Integrate diversity, equity, and inclusion as core to one's professional identity.

description: Engages in career-long learning and leads within professional nursing practice and the healthcare environment.

competencies.text: Demonstrate a commitment to personal health and well-being.
Foster a positive work environment.
Demonstrate a spirit of inquiry that fosters flexibility and professional maturity.
Develop capacity for leadership.
